headline: Southern’s US$26.5b DOE Loan Reshapes Debt Profile And Investor Focus
summary: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) has secured a US$26.5b loan agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy. The financing is described as historic for the company and is expected to support long-term customer savings. The loan is also expected to ease pressure on capital markets funding for Southern Company. Southern Company, a major U.S. utility with a focus on power generation and regulated electric and gas distribution, now has a fresh funding source of unusual scale. For investors watching...
